# llms-full.txt for Context Rot: A Journal of Computational Decay

## About this blog
Context Rot is a satirical technical blog that explores computational decay and AI context management through academic-style papers. It combines rigorous technical analysis with humor and commentary on the state of AI development.

## Topics covered
- AI context management and decay
- Configuration schema proliferation
- Developer cognitive load
- AI agent interoperability
- Computational entropy
- Software architecture critiques
- AI system design patterns

## Content structure
- Academic-style research papers
- ArXiv-inspired formatting
- Interactive commenting system
- Line-specific feedback mechanism
- Technical satire and commentary

## Key concepts
- Context Rot: The inevitable degradation of context in AI systems
- Faux-moat construction: Creating artificial barriers through proprietary standards
- Configuration Schema Entropy (CSE): Disorder from unnecessary format diversification
- Developer Cognitive Load (DCL): Mental overhead from multiple configuration formats
- MD_FILES framework: A satirical solution to configuration proliferation

## Author information
Dr. B. Prop - Principal Investigator at the Context Decay Research Institute, a fictional character representing the academic voice of the blog.

## Interactive features
- Select any text to leave comments
- Contribute to research through feedback
- AI-powered feedback integration system
- Community-driven content improvement

## Technical details
- Built with Jekyll static site generator
- ArXiv-style CSS for academic appearance
- Privacy-friendly analytics
- RSS feed available
- GitHub integration
- Mobile-responsive design

## Related resources
- GitHub repository: https://github.com/context-rot/blogistan
- MD_FILES Framework: https://context-rot.github.io/md-files-framework
- Twitter: @context_rot

## Contact
Email: editor@context-rot.com
